site.name: Gilt
site.mechanism_justification: Only UC Chrome with residential proxy can bypass the site's anti-bot protection. Standard HTTP and standard Playwright browser are both blocked. The page is heavily JS-rendered — all common product selectors (price, title, availability, SKU, etc.) return NOT FOUND, indicating data loads via JavaScript after page load. JSON-LD provides partial product data (name, image, description, brand) but has no offers/price information. Full browser rendering via UC Chrome is required to extract complete product data from the DOM.

anti_bot.details: Standard HTTP requests and standard Playwright browser are blocked. Only UC Chrome with residential proxy (uc_chrome_residential) successfully loads the page. The anti-bot system is not Cloudflare/Akamai/PerimeterX (no standard markers detected) but effectively blocks non-UC browser access.

connectivity.notes: Standard HTTP and standard Playwright browser are blocked. Only uc_chrome_residential works. Residential proxy is required — datacenter proxy would likely also be blocked.

product_discovery.url_pattern_notes: Product URLs follow the pattern /boutique/product/{boutique_id}/{product_id}/ where boutique_id is a numeric category/brand identifier and product_id is a unique numeric product identifier. Tracking query parameters (campaignid, gclid, etc.) are appended but are not required for page access.

data_extraction.notes: JSON-LD Product block is present but lacks offers/price data. Price, availability, variants, and sizing information must be extracted from the rendered DOM. All common CSS selectors for price/availability/title return NOT FOUND, meaning the site uses custom class names. The scraper will need to use UC Chrome to render the page and then inspect the DOM for custom selectors (e.g., data-testid attributes, specific class patterns).

rate_limiting.justification: High anti-bot protection requiring residential proxy — moderate delays to avoid detection.
